strategy: Prepare for next session — optimize scanner to catch early movers, favor gap-up momentum with volume confirmation

parameter_suggestions.reason: Zero sentiment threshold too permissive — need bullish bias filter to avoid chop trades
Risk tier already at 7% — align position sizing to match, maximize capital velocity on first wins
5min scan too slow for momentum plays — need faster pulse at market open to catch breakouts

key_insight: Day 1, hour 0 — the only risk right now is NOT trading. Scanner criteria are likely too tight or missing volume/volatility filters. When market opens, we need to be HUNTING not spectating. First trade is psychological — take it clean, take profit fast, build momentum. Empty scanner after-hours is fine, but if this persists into first 30min of regular session, we have a sourcing problem not a strategy problem. Adjust thresholds NOW so we're ready to pounce at 9:30 ET.

strategy: Pre-market preparation mode: identify high-probability first trade candidates to deploy capital at open and establish psychological momentum.

parameter_suggestions.reason: First trade needs quick win for psychological momentum - tighter TP1 increases probability of banking green, then let TP2 run
With zero track record, first trade must be high-conviction - raise bar slightly to avoid weak setups
At market open (9:30-10:00 ET), scan every 60s to catch momentum breakouts - capital velocity demands speed

key_insight: The first trade is disproportionately important. It sets psychological tone and validates the entire system. Focus tomorrow's open on ONE high-probability setup: liquid mega-cap (AAPL/MSFT/NVDA/TSLA) with clear technical setup + positive momentum. Get $70 deployed fast, bank TP1 at 1.2%, establish the win streak counter. Dead capital is the enemy - deploy by 10:00 AM ET or accept you're losing 6 hours of compounding opportunity. Pre-market: monitor futures, identify which sector is leading, have 3 symbols ready to scan hard at 9:30 bell.

strategy: Pre-market prep mode: tighten scanner criteria, bias toward high-volume gappers at open, then execute aggressively to break the zero-velocity deadlock

parameter_suggestions.reason: Lower barrier for first trades - we need DATA to optimize from, can't tune a silent system
Market open will be fast - need higher scan frequency to catch momentum before it's priced in
Start tighter to GUARANTEE early wins and build confidence data - can expand once we have proof of execution

key_insight: You're in analysis paralysis limbo. The biggest risk isn't losing 1.5% on a bad trade - it's burning entire trading days at 0% velocity. At market open (9:30 ET), prioritize EXECUTION over perfection: take the first 2-3 qualified setups even if sentiment is borderline. We need game film to learn from. A small loss teaches more than perfect idle cash. Target: 3+ trades in first 90 minutes tomorrow to kickstart the feedback loop.
